results.title: Had to kill my favorite feature to survive Apple Review ✂️ (Referral ...
results.content:
Emre Yılmaz

Man, that 'Invite a Friend' rejection is a classic Apple Review heartbreak. We've all been there. Since direct premium rewards are a no go for them, have you thought about moving the referral logic entirely to a web dashboard? You can reward users with credits or extended trials through a web login which Apple usually complains less about since it's not strictly 'in app' unlocking. Thanks for sharing the pain point, definitely choosing shipping over perfection was the right call!

Orhan Kilic
OptiClear

OptiClear [...] Alper Tayfur

Oof, painful but very relatable 😅

Apple’s review rules are pretty strict here — if premium digital features are unlocked in-app, they generally want that value to flow through In-App Purchase, not side-door referral rewards. Their guidelines around premium features and subscriptions make that pretty clear.

A safer path is usually:

reward with something non-premium / cosmetic

use Apple’s own offer codes or promo-style subscription discounts

or move the referral benefit outside the app experience

So yeah, shipping over perfection was probably the right call here. The feature can come back later in a form Apple is happier with.

Orhan Kilic
OptiClear

OptiClear [...] I’d love to know your strategies:   
  
1️⃣ Has anyone successfully implemented a referral/reward system that Apple actually approves?   
  
2️⃣ If not, what are your best alternative strategies for organic growth without relying on invite loops?

Let’s share some App Store survival tactics! 👇

30

101

Add a comment

### Replies

Stan Kolotinskiy

That's a painful call to make, especially when you can see the feature working exactly as intended. Apple's review process has this way of killing things that are genuinely good for users just because they don't fit the kinda weird economical model designed by them. From the other side, having a simple "share this with someone" prompt without any reward attached could make people happy as well!

Orhan Kilic
OptiClear

OptiClear

results.title: Roame: Fly for free using your points & miles | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

 24 views

Tim Qin

Roame

Roame

!!!! That’s a next level redemption. Where to?

Report

•1 review

Incredible concept here - optimizing all the various reward currencies with route availability is one of those time sinks that tends to over-complicate and spoil the planning process of upcoming trips for me. Definitely impressed to see point transfers between partners included in the search results. It'd be cool if roame could help more seamlessly facilitate those transfers as well someday? One of my stress points is hoping that a ticket will still be available sometimes several days later when a point transfer completes and becomes available to spend. Excited to see this one develop over the coming months. Congrats on the launch & good luck to the Roame team!

#### What's great [...] Roame Travel

Roame TravelFly for free using your points

Launched on January 27th, 2025

Roame

RoameFly for free using your points & miles

Launched on July 10th, 2023

4.9

Based on 16 reviews

Review Roame?

Reviewers say Roame makes award-flight search much easier by pulling options across airlines and loyalty programs into one place, with a clean interface, fast results, and clear views of transfer paths and out-of-pocket fees. Several users say it saves hours of manual comparison and has helped them find and book strong redemptions, including business-class trips, while surfacing value they would have missed otherwise. The main caveat is clarity: a few reviewers found parts of the experience not fully transparent or still a bit hard to understand.

+13

Summarized with AI

Pros [...] Cons

not always transparent(2)

•1 review

Best in class. I've saved over 10k on flights. E.g. - Memphis to London Business Class retailed for 7k USD on American Airlines. Noticed on my paid Roame account that Alaska also supported the flight for 45k points. Alaska was also doing a deal on points which retailed $480 + $200 cash fee. - Saved another 3k on an Air France Business class flight from London to Singapore. Retailed for 2.5k each way, got each flight for just about 1k when transferring points with my Amex and doing the point equivalent calculations. The value is clear.

#### What's great

award flight search engine (7)maximize points value (3)award travel deals (2)cost-effective travel (1)points transfer strategies (2)
